---
title: Node Classification
subtitle: Classifying and snapping WFRC network nodes to physical centerline endpoints
description: WFRC MasterNet node conflation — snapping abstract model nodes to UGRC/UDOT physical centerlines
author:
 - name: Pukar Bhandari
   email: pukar.bhandari@wfrc.utah.gov
   affiliation:
     - name: Wasatch Front Regional Council
       url: "https://wfrc.utah.gov/"
date: "2026-04-03"
---

## Setup

### Imports

```{python}
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import folium
import geopandas as gpd
import gtfs_kit as gk
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import shapely
from shapely.geometry import LineString
from shapely.strtree import STRtree

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd()))
from _src.node_utils import (
    nodes_on,
    count_links,
    assign_node_directions,
    assign_endpoint_directions,
    snap_nodes,
    snap_transit,
)
```

### Configuration

```{python}
# ── Filepaths ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATHS = {
    "in_centerlines":      Path("_data/raw/BangerterEdits.gdb.zip"),
    "in_network_links":    Path("_data/raw/WFv1000_MasterNet - Link.gpkg"),
    "in_network_nodes":    Path("_data/raw/WFv1000_MasterNet - Node.gpkg"),
    "in_gtfs":             Path("_data/raw/uta_gtfs.zip"),
    "out_node_classified": Path("_data/processed/nodes_classified.gpkg"),
    "out_node_snapped":    Path("_data/processed/nodes_snapped.gpkg"),
    "out_debug":           Path("_data/processed/debug_snapping.gpkg"),
}

# ── CRS ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CRS_UTM = "EPSG:26912"   # NAD83 UTM Zone 12N — Utah (metric)
CRS_WGS = "EPSG:4326"    # WGS84 — required by Folium
```

---

## Load Data

### Roadway Centerlines

```{python}
COUNTY_FIPS = ["49003", "49057", "49011", "49035", "49049"]
county_filter = "(" + ", ".join(f"'{f}'" for f in COUNTY_FIPS) + ")"

gdf_centerlines = gpd.read_file(
    f"/vsizip/{PATHS['in_centerlines']}",
    layer="RoadsBangerterEtc",
    where=f"COUNTY_L IN {county_filter} OR COUNTY_R IN {county_filter}",
).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

# Strip trailing whitespace from categorical fields (source-data artefact)
for col in ["DOT_FCLASS", "CARTOCODE", "STATUS", "ONEWAY", "VERT_LEVEL", "POSTTYPE"]:
    if col in gdf_centerlines.columns:
        gdf_centerlines[col] = gdf_centerlines[col].str.strip()

print(f"Centerlines: {len(gdf_centerlines):,} features | CRS: {gdf_centerlines.crs}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
gdf_centerlines.head(1000).explore()
```

### Network Links and Nodes

```{python}
# Only links active in the 2027 network (LN_2027 > 0)
gdf_links = gpd.read_file(PATHS["in_network_links"], where="LN_2027 > 0").to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_nodes = gpd.read_file(PATHS["in_network_nodes"]).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

print(f"Links: {len(gdf_links):,} (LN_2027 > 0)")
print(f"Nodes: {len(gdf_nodes):,}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
gdf_links.head(1000).explore()
gdf_nodes.head(1000).explore()
```

### GTFS Rail Stops

Filter GTFS stops to Light Rail (TRAX, route_type 0) and Commuter Rail
(FrontRunner, route_type 2) only, walking the routes → trips → stop_times chain.

```{python}
feed = gk.read_feed(PATHS["in_gtfs"], dist_units="km")

gdf_stops  = gk.get_stops(feed,  as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_shapes = gk.get_shapes(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_routes = gk.get_routes(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

RAIL_ROUTE_TYPES = {0, 2}   # 0 = Light Rail (TRAX), 2 = Commuter Rail (FrontRunner)

rail_route_ids = feed.routes.loc[feed.routes["route_type"].isin(RAIL_ROUTE_TYPES), "route_id"]
rail_trip_ids  = feed.trips.loc[feed.trips["route_id"].isin(rail_route_ids), "trip_id"]
rail_stop_ids  = feed.stop_times.loc[feed.stop_times["trip_id"].isin(rail_trip_ids), "stop_id"].unique()

gdf_rail_stops = gdf_stops[gdf_stops["stop_id"].isin(rail_stop_ids)].copy()
print(f"Rail stops: {len(gdf_rail_stops):,} of {len(gdf_stops):,} total stops")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
gdf_rail_stops.explore()
```

---

## Part A — Origin Processing (Network Nodes)

### A1 — Functional Type Flags

`nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, ft_codes)` returns True for any node whose
N appears as endpoint A or B of at least one link whose FT code is in `ft_codes`.

Flags are **non-exclusive** — a node touching both a freeway and a ramp link
will be True for both `Freeway` and `Ramp`. Mutual exclusion is enforced later
in the topology cascade (§A3).

```{python}
# ── FT code groups (2027 network year) ────────────────────────────────────
#
# FT_2027 code reference
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Freeway mainline (FW_CODES):
#   12-15  Expressway (limited-access, divided, no full interchange control)
#   22-26  MM freeway (posted 55-75 mph, MM series)
#   32-36  Freeway    (posted 55-75 mph, Fwy series)
#   Note: 21 (MM CD road) and 31 (Fwy CD road) are excluded — they are
#         physically collector-distributor geometry, not mainline.
#
# Ramp / CD (RAMP_CODES):
#   30     FwyLoopRamp / 20 MMLoopRamp — fwy-to-fwy connectors with mainline geometry
#   28/29  MM on/off ramps  |  41/42  Fwy on/off ramps
#   21/31  CD roads (merged with ramps — FT distinction is unreliable in practice)
#
# Surface (SURFACE_CODES):
#   2/3  Principal/Minor Arterial  |  4/5  Major/Minor Collector  |  6/7  Local/Unpaved
#
# HOV / Managed lanes (HOV_CODES):
#   37 HOV lane  |  38 Managed lane  |  39 Managed-lane access point
#   Excluded from FW_CODES: HOT lane geometry does not coincide with mainline endpoints.
#
# Fixed transit:
#   70 Light Rail (TRAX)  |  80 Commuter Rail (FrontRunner)
#   N ranges 10000-19999 and 50000-59999: purpose-built station nodes with no road geometry.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FW_CODES = set(range(22, 27)) | set(range(32, 37)) | {12, 13, 14, 15}
RAMP_CODES = {28, 29, 41, 42, 21, 31, 20, 30}
SURFACE_CODES = {2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7}
HOV_CODES = {37, 38, 39}

gdf_nodes["Freeway"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, FW_CODES)
gdf_nodes["Ramp"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, RAMP_CODES)
gdf_nodes["Surface"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, SURFACE_CODES)
gdf_nodes["HOV"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, HOV_CODES)
gdf_nodes["FixedTransit"] = (
    nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, {70, 80})
    | gdf_nodes["N"].between(10_000, 19_999)  # TRAX station nodes
    | gdf_nodes["N"].between(50_000, 59_999)  # FrontRunner station nodes
)

flag_cols = ["Freeway", "Ramp", "Surface", "HOV", "FixedTransit"]
print("Flag counts:")
print(gdf_nodes[flag_cols].sum().rename("NodeCount").to_frame().to_string())
```

### A2 — Link Count and Direction

`count_links` adds `LinkCount` — the number of link-endpoint references (A or B)
that touch each node. Nodes with LinkCount == 2 are **pseudonodes** (shape
vertices); they have no physical counterpart and are excluded from all snapping.

`assign_node_directions` produces two direction columns (see docstring):
- `link_directions` — freeway-priority cascade; used in passes (a) and (c).
- `fw_directions`   — freeway links only, majority-vote; used in pass (b) gore nodes.

```{python}
gdf_nodes = count_links(gdf_nodes, gdf_links)
gdf_nodes = assign_node_directions(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, freeway_ft_codes=FW_CODES)

print(f"Pseudonodes (LinkCount==2): {(gdf_nodes['LinkCount'] == 2).sum():,}")
```

```{python}
# Link-count distribution chart
link_count_freq = gdf_nodes["LinkCount"].value_counts().sort_index()
max_val = link_count_freq.max()

BASE_COLOR   = "#B4B2A9"
ACCENT_COLOR = "#1D9E75"
PSEUDO_COLOR = "#EF9F27"
LABEL_COLOR  = "#444441"
GRID_COLOR   = "#D3D1C7"

bar_colors = [
    PSEUDO_COLOR if lc == 2 else (ACCENT_COLOR if lc in {4, 6, 8} else BASE_COLOR)
    for lc in link_count_freq.index
]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5.5))
fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")
ax.set_facecolor("white")
ax.yaxis.grid(True, color=GRID_COLOR, linewidth=0.6, zorder=0)
ax.set_axisbelow(True)

bars = ax.bar(link_count_freq.index, link_count_freq.values, color=bar_colors, width=0.75, zorder=2)
for bar in bars:
    h = bar.get_height()
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2, h + max_val * 0.012,
            f"{int(h):,}", ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=9.5, color=LABEL_COLOR)

for spine in ["top", "right", "left"]:
    ax.spines[spine].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["bottom"].set_color(GRID_COLOR)
ax.set_xticks(link_count_freq.index)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{int(x):,}"))
ax.tick_params(axis="y", labelsize=9.5, colors="#888780", length=0)
ax.tick_params(axis="x", length=0)
ax.set_ylim(0, max_val * 1.18)
ax.set_xlabel("Number of links connected to node", fontsize=10.5, color="#888780", labelpad=10)

ax.legend(
    [plt.Rectangle((0,0),1,1,color=c) for c in [ACCENT_COLOR, PSEUDO_COLOR, BASE_COLOR]],
    ["Typical intersection (4, 6, 8)", "Pseudonode (2)", "Other"],
    loc="upper right", fontsize=9, frameon=False, labelcolor="#888780",
)
fig.text(0.13, 1.01, "Most nodes connect to 4, 6, or 8 links",
         fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", color=LABEL_COLOR, transform=ax.transAxes)
fig.text(0.13, 0.94, f"Distribution of {len(gdf_nodes):,} network nodes by link count",
         fontsize=10.5, color="#888780", transform=ax.transAxes)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### A3 — Node Topology Classification

Three non-exclusive boolean masks → five mutually-exclusive `node_type` labels
via priority cascade (freeway > ramp > surface). HOV excluded from `is_fwy`
because HOT lane geometry does not coincide with mainline endpoints.
Pseudonodes (LinkCount == 2) are shape vertices with no physical counterpart.

```{python}
no_pseudo = gdf_nodes["LinkCount"] != 2

is_fwy_node     = gdf_nodes["Freeway"] & ~gdf_nodes["HOV"] & no_pseudo
is_ramp_node    = gdf_nodes["Ramp"]    & no_pseudo
is_surface_node = gdf_nodes["Surface"] & no_pseudo

gdf_nodes["node_type"] = np.select(
    [
        is_fwy_node  & ~is_ramp_node,                   # pure mainline
        is_fwy_node  &  is_ramp_node,                   # mainline/ramp junction
        ~is_fwy_node &  is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node,# mid-ramp
        ~is_fwy_node &  is_ramp_node &  is_surface_node,# ramp-to-arterial terminal
    ],
    ["fwy", "gore", "ramp", "ramp_sf"],
    default="surface",
)

print("Node type distribution:")
print(gdf_nodes["node_type"].value_counts().rename("count").to_frame().to_string())
```

```{python}
# Optional: inspect node flags and directions
gdf_nodes[["N", "LinkCount", "node_type", "link_directions", "fw_directions"]].head(20)
```

```{python}
out_path = PATHS["out_node_classified"]
out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_nodes.to_crs(CRS_WGS).to_file(out_path, driver="GPKG")
print(f"Classified nodes saved to: {out_path}")
```

---

## Part B — Target Processing (Centerline Endpoints)

The endpoint layer makes topology and direction assignments explicit and
inspectable in QGIS **before** any snapping happens. The full workflow is:
B1 filter to active segments → B2 classify segments → B3 extract endpoints →
B4 deduplicate → B5 assign directions → B6 assign ep_type → B7 export.

### B1 — Filter to Active Segments

```{python}
# Exclude retired and planned segments — active physical geometry only
active_cl = gdf_centerlines[~gdf_centerlines["STATUS"].isin(["Retired", "Planned"])].copy()
print(f"Active centerlines: {len(active_cl):,} of {len(gdf_centerlines):,}")
```

### B2 — Classify Segments

Three non-exclusive boolean flags per segment. Classification authority is
`DOT_RTNAME` (UDOT LRS), not `DOT_FCLASS` (inconsistently coded across vintages).

```
DOT_RTNAME layout (11 characters, zero-padded):
  [0:4]  Route number      e.g. "0015" = I-15, "0154" = Bangerter
  [4]    LRS direction     "P" = primary (NB/EB),  "N" = negative (SB/WB)
  [5]    Facility type     " " = mainline, "R" = ramp, "C" = collector-distributor
  [6:]   Sequence/suffix   varies
```

These flags flow into every endpoint pool and type-tier decision, making this
the **primary place to audit** when snapping results look wrong.

```{python}
# Controlled-access corridors in the Wasatch Front study area.
# Adding/removing a route here is the primary tuning lever for freeway coverage.
FREEWAY_ROUTES = {
    "0015",   # Interstate 15         (primary N-S spine)
    "0067",   # Legacy Parkway        (limited-access, no ramps)
    "0080",   # Interstate 80         (E-W, SLC to Parley's Canyon)
    "0084",   # Interstate 84         (Ogden corridor)
    "0089",   # US-89                 (surface in places — watch for mis-classification)
    "0092",   # Timpanogos Highway    (SR-92, American Fork Canyon)
    "0154",   # Bangerter Highway     (semi-controlled ring road)
    "0177",   # West Davis Corridor   (new build, limited segments)
    "0201",   # SR-201                (West Valley connector)
    "0215",   # Interstate 215        (belt route, SLC)
}

rtname = active_cl["DOT_RTNAME"].str

# is_freeway: mainline segments only.
#   Route must be in FREEWAY_ROUTES AND facility char must not be "R" (ramp) or "C" (CD).
#   Ramp/CD segments share the parent route number — without these exclusions they
#   inflate the freeway pool and corrupt gore detection.
active_cl["is_freeway"] = (
    rtname.slice(0, 4).isin(FREEWAY_ROUTES)
    & (rtname.slice(5, 6) != "R")
    & (rtname.slice(5, 6) != "C")
)

# is_interchange: ramp and CD segments.
#   DOT_RTNAME[5] == "R" : official UDOT ramp (on/off ramps, loop ramps).
#   DOT_RTNAME[5] == "C" : collector-distributor roads.
#   POSTTYPE == "RAMP"   : short local slip ramps without a full 11-char LRS name.
#   Note: non-exclusive with is_freeway intentionally — a gore segment may carry both.
active_cl["is_interchange"] = (
    (rtname.slice(5, 6) == "R")
    | (rtname.slice(5, 6) == "C")
    | (active_cl["POSTTYPE"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper() == "RAMP")
)

# is_surface: residual — everything that is neither freeway mainline nor interchange.
active_cl["is_surface"] = ~active_cl["is_freeway"] & ~active_cl["is_interchange"]

print("Segment-level classification:")
print(f"  is_freeway     (mainline):   {active_cl['is_freeway'].sum():,}")
print(f"  is_interchange (ramp / CD):  {active_cl['is_interchange'].sum():,}")
print(f"  is_surface     (all others): {active_cl['is_surface'].sum():,}")
print(f"  both fwy + ic  (gore segs):  {(active_cl['is_freeway'] & active_cl['is_interchange']).sum():,}")
print(f"  total active:                {len(active_cl):,}")
```

### B3 — Extract Raw Endpoints

Extract the start and end point of every active segment into a flat GeoDataFrame
(two rows per segment). Carries `FULLNAME`, `DOT_RTNAME`, and the three topology
flags forward for direction resolution and deduplication downstream.

```{python}
# Explode MultiLineString → LineString so get_point works correctly
active_cl = active_cl.explode(index_parts=False).reset_index(drop=True)
print(
    f"After explode: {len(active_cl):,} segments (was {len(gdf_centerlines[~gdf_centerlines['STATUS'].isin(['Retired', 'Planned'])]):,})"
)

starts = shapely.get_point(active_cl.geometry.values, 0)
ends = shapely.get_point(active_cl.geometry.values, -1)
print(f"Null starts after explode: {shapely.is_missing(starts).sum()}")  # should be 0

xs_all = np.concatenate([shapely.get_x(starts), shapely.get_x(ends)])
ys_all = np.concatenate([shapely.get_y(starts), shapely.get_y(ends)])
tile = lambda col: np.concatenate([active_cl[col].values, active_cl[col].values])

gdf_ep_raw = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    {
        "geometry": np.concatenate([starts, ends]),
        "seg_idx": tile("index")
        if "index" in active_cl.columns
        else np.concatenate([active_cl.index.values, active_cl.index.values]),
        "x_round": np.round(xs_all, 2),
        "y_round": np.round(ys_all, 2),
        "fullname": tile("FULLNAME"),
        "dot_rtname": tile("DOT_RTNAME"),
        "is_freeway": tile("is_freeway"),
        "is_interchange": tile("is_interchange"),
        "is_surface": tile("is_surface"),
        "is_start": np.concatenate([np.ones(len(active_cl), bool), np.zeros(len(active_cl), bool)]),
    },
    crs=CRS_UTM,
).reset_index(drop=True)

print(f"Raw endpoint records: {len(gdf_ep_raw):,}  (2 × {len(active_cl):,} segments)")
```

### B4 — Deduplicate by Coordinate

Group raw endpoints by rounded XY (millimetre precision). Aggregate type flags
with OR — a coordinate is `is_freeway=True` if any freeway segment terminates there.

```{python}
ep_agg = gdf_ep_raw.groupby(["x_round", "y_round"], as_index=False).agg(
    is_freeway=("is_freeway", "any"),
    is_interchange=("is_interchange", "any"),
    is_surface=("is_surface", "any"),
)
ep_agg["geometry"] = gpd.points_from_xy(ep_agg["x_round"], ep_agg["y_round"])
gdf_ep_unique = gpd.GeoDataFrame(ep_agg, crs=CRS_UTM)

print(f"Unique endpoints: {len(gdf_ep_unique):,}")
print(
    f"  freeway-only   : {(gdf_ep_unique['is_freeway'] & ~gdf_ep_unique['is_interchange']).sum():,}"
)
print(
    f"  freeway + ramp : {(gdf_ep_unique['is_freeway'] & gdf_ep_unique['is_interchange']).sum():,}"
)
print(
    f"  ramp-only      : {(~gdf_ep_unique['is_freeway'] & gdf_ep_unique['is_interchange'] & ~gdf_ep_unique['is_surface']).sum():,}"
)
print(f"  surface        : {gdf_ep_unique['is_surface'].sum():,}")
```

### B5 — Assign Endpoint Directions

`assign_endpoint_directions` calls `_resolve_direction` per unique coordinate,
aggregating direction from all contributing raw segments with freeway-first priority.

```{python}
gdf_ep_unique = assign_endpoint_directions(gdf_ep_unique, gdf_ep_raw)

print(f"Endpoints with direction: {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_allowed_dirs'] != '').sum():,}")
print(f"Endpoints direction-agnostic: {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_allowed_dirs'] == '').sum():,}")
```

### B6 — Assign Endpoint Type

Six mutually-exclusive `ep_type` labels derived from the three boolean flags,
mirroring the `node_type` cascade so type-tier matching compares like with like.
`gore` is checked first — a point where freeway and ramp both terminate is always
a gore regardless of surface contact.

```{python}
fw = gdf_ep_unique["is_freeway"]
ic = gdf_ep_unique["is_interchange"]
sf = gdf_ep_unique["is_surface"]

gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] = np.select(
    [
        fw &  ic,            # freeway/ramp junction
        fw &  sf,            # at-grade freeway/surface crossing or frontage
        fw & ~ic & ~sf,      # pure freeway mainline endpoint
        ~fw & ic &  sf,      # ramp-to-arterial terminal
        ~fw & ic & ~sf,      # mid-ramp endpoint
    ],
    ["gore", "fwy_sf", "fwy", "ramp_sf", "ramp"],
    default="surface",
)

print("Endpoint type distribution:")
print(gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"].value_counts().rename("count").to_frame().to_string())
```

```{python}
gdf_ep_unique[["x_round", "y_round", "is_freeway", "is_interchange", "is_surface",
               "ep_allowed_dirs", "ep_type"]].head(20)
```

### B7 — Export Endpoint Layer for QGIS Inspection

Verify before running any snapping:
- Freeway-only endpoints correctly identified (no ramp/CD bleeds)
- Gore endpoints at physical mainline/ramp junctions
- Direction strings match expected cardinal for each location

```{python}
debug_path = PATHS["out_debug"]
debug_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_ep_unique.to_crs(CRS_WGS).to_file(debug_path, layer="endpoint_layer", driver="GPKG")  # reproject to WGS84 for export only
print(f"Endpoint layer → {debug_path} / layer='endpoint_layer'")
```

---

## Part C — Interchange Node Direction Enrichment

For interchange-only nodes (pass c), the directional signal is inherited one hop
from adjacent freeway mainline links. This enrichment is done **explicitly here**
(not hidden inside `snap_nodes`) so the lookup table is inspectable.

```{python}
# Build a flat edge list: (node_N, FT, DIRECTION) — vectorised via pd.concat
edges = pd.concat([
    gdf_links[["A", "FT_2027", "DIRECTION"]].rename(columns={"A": "N"}),
    gdf_links[["B", "FT_2027", "DIRECTION"]].rename(columns={"B": "N"}),
], ignore_index=True)
edges["is_freeway_link"] = edges["FT_2027"].isin(FW_CODES)
edges["DIRECTION"]       = edges["DIRECTION"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()

CARDINAL = {"NB", "SB", "EB", "WB"}

# For each interchange-only node, collect cardinals from adjacent freeway links.
ic_only_mask = ~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node
ic_only_Ns   = set(gdf_nodes.loc[ic_only_mask, "N"])

adj_fw = (
    edges[edges["N"].isin(ic_only_Ns) & edges["is_freeway_link"] & edges["DIRECTION"].isin(CARDINAL)]
    .groupby("N")["DIRECTION"]
    .apply(lambda s: ",".join(sorted(set(s))))
)

gdf_nodes["ic_fw_directions"] = gdf_nodes["N"].map(adj_fw).fillna("")

resolved = (gdf_nodes.loc[ic_only_mask, "ic_fw_directions"] != "").sum()
total_ic  = ic_only_mask.sum()
print(f"Interchange-only nodes with freeway-neighbor direction: {resolved:,} of {total_ic:,}")
print(f"  remaining {total_ic - resolved:,} are direction-agnostic (no adjacent freeway link)")
```

---

## Part D — Node Snapping

Hierarchical execution — first-call-wins. Nodes snapped in an earlier pass are
skipped by subsequent passes.

### D1 — Freeway and Interchange Snapping

Three passes in topology-priority order.

```{python}
# ── Endpoint pools (by ep_type) ───────────────────────────────────────────
ep_fwy_only = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "fwy"].copy()
ep_gore     = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "gore"].copy()
ep_ic_only  = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"].isin(["ramp", "ramp_sf"])].copy()

print(f"Pass (a) endpoint pool — fwy only:       {len(ep_fwy_only):,}")
print(f"Pass (b) endpoint pool — gore:           {len(ep_gore):,}")
print(f"Pass (c) endpoint pool — ramp + ramp_sf: {len(ep_ic_only):,}")

# ── Node masks ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print()
print(f"Pass (a)  nodes — freeway-only: {(is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node).sum():,}")
print(f"Pass (b)  nodes — gore:         {(is_fwy_node &  is_ramp_node).sum():,}")
print(f"Pass (c)  nodes — pure ramp:    {(~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node).sum():,}")
print(f"  ramp_sf nodes → surface pass: {(~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node &  is_surface_node).sum():,}")
```

```{python}
# ── Pre-snap distance check ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Confirm coverage and validate distance thresholds before committing.
fwy_ic_eligible = gdf_nodes[is_fwy_node | is_ramp_node]
all_fwy_ep = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["is_freeway"] | gdf_ep_unique["is_interchange"]]

ep_geoms   = all_fwy_ep.geometry.to_numpy()
node_geoms = fwy_ic_eligible.geometry.to_numpy()

tree_pre     = STRtree(ep_geoms)
nearest_idxs = tree_pre.nearest(node_geoms)
pre_dists    = shapely.distance(node_geoms, ep_geoms[nearest_idxs])

print("Nearest endpoint distance — all freeway + interchange eligible nodes:")
print(f"  min:    {pre_dists.min():.1f}m")
print(f"  median: {np.median(pre_dists):.1f}m")
print(f"  p75:    {np.percentile(pre_dists, 75):.1f}m")
print(f"  p95:    {np.percentile(pre_dists, 95):.1f}m")
print(f"  max:    {pre_dists.max():.1f}m")
print(f"  ≤  50m: {(pre_dists <= 50).sum():,}  ({(pre_dists <= 50).mean() * 100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  ≤ 150m: {(pre_dists <= 150).sum():,}  ({(pre_dists <= 150).mean() * 100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  > 300m: {(pre_dists > 300).sum():,}  ({(pre_dists > 300).mean() * 100:.0f}%)")
```

```{python}
gdf_nodes_snapped = gdf_nodes.copy()

# Pass (a) — Freeway-only nodes → fwy-only endpoint pool
#   Type tier: fwy ep = Tier 0,  gore/fwy_sf ep = Tier 1.
#   Direction: link_directions (freeway-priority cascade).
#   Threshold: 500m (freeway geometry can sit well off the model centroid).
gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    ep_fwy_only,
    node_mask=is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node,
    max_distance_m=500,
    label="Freeway_Only",
    direction_col="link_directions",
    target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
)

# Pass (b) — Gore nodes → gore endpoint pool
#   Type tier: gore ep = Tier 0,  fwy/fwy_sf/ramp ep = Tier 1.
#   Direction: fw_directions (freeway side of the mixed node only —
#              isolates mainline direction, ignoring ramp/CD link directions).
#   Threshold: 500m.
gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    ep_gore,
    node_mask=is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node,
    max_distance_m=500,
    label="Freeway_Gore",
    direction_col="fw_directions",
    target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
)

# Pass (c) — Pure ramp nodes (mid-ramp, no surface contact) → ramp endpoint pool
#   Direction: ic_fw_directions (inherited one hop from adjacent freeway — see Part C).
#   Pure ramp nodes have no surface links so link_directions is unreliable;
#   the freeway-neighbor direction is the best available signal.
#   Threshold: 100m (mid-ramp nodes sit close to physical endpoints).
#   ramp_sf nodes (ramp + surface) are intentionally excluded here — they route
#   directly to the surface pass (D2) which uses link_directions and a wider threshold.
gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    ep_ic_only,
    node_mask=~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node,
    max_distance_m=100,
    label="Interchange_Only",
    direction_col="ic_fw_directions",
    target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
)
```

### D1 Summary

```{python}
FWY_IC_RULES = ["Freeway_Only", "Freeway_Gore", "Interchange_Only"]

for rule in FWY_IC_RULES:
    dists = gdf_nodes_snapped.loc[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == rule, "snap_distance_m"]
    if len(dists) == 0:
        print(f"{rule}: 0 nodes snapped")
        continue
    print(f"{rule} ({len(dists):,} nodes):")
    print(f"  median {dists.median():.1f}m  |  p90 {dists.quantile(0.9):.1f}m  |  max {dists.max():.1f}m")
    print(f"  < 50m: {(dists < 50).sum():,}  ({(dists < 50).mean()*100:.0f}%)")

exceeded_fwy_ic = gdf_nodes_snapped[
    (is_fwy_node | is_ramp_node) & (gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold")
]
print(f"\nNodes exceeding threshold (all passes): {len(exceeded_fwy_ic):,}")
if len(exceeded_fwy_ic) > 0:
    print(exceeded_fwy_ic["node_type"].value_counts().rename("count").to_frame().to_string())
    recycled = (exceeded_fwy_ic["node_type"] == "ramp_sf").sum()
    print(f"\nramp_sf recycling to Surface pass: {recycled:,}")
    print("  (Expected — ramp-to-arterial terminals; surface ep is Tier-0 for them)")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

# Folium validation map — Freeway + Interchange snapping
fwy_snapped  = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"].isin(FWY_IC_RULES)]
fwy_original = gdf_nodes.loc[fwy_snapped.index]

fwy_snapped_wgs  = fwy_snapped.to_crs(CRS_WGS)
fwy_original_wgs = fwy_original.to_crs(CRS_WGS)

connectors = [
    {"geometry": LineString([fwy_original_wgs.geometry.loc[idx], fwy_snapped_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]]),
     "N": int(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "N"]),
     "dist_m": float(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_distance_m"])}
    for idx in fwy_snapped.index
    if fwy_original_wgs.geometry.loc[idx].distance(fwy_snapped_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]) > 0
]
gdf_fwy_connectors = gpd.GeoDataFrame(connectors, crs=CRS_WGS)

RULE_COLORS = {"Freeway_Only": "#1D9E75", "Freeway_Gore": "#534AB7", "Interchange_Only": "#EF9F27"}

center = [fwy_original_wgs.geometry.y.mean(), fwy_original_wgs.geometry.x.mean()]
m = folium.Map(location=center, zoom_start=11, tiles="CartoDB positron")

conn_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Displacement lines", show=True)
for _, row in gdf_fwy_connectors.iterrows():
    folium.PolyLine([(c[1], c[0]) for c in row.geometry.coords],
                    color="#888780", weight=1, opacity=0.7,
                    tooltip=f"N: {row['N']} | {row['dist_m']}m").add_to(conn_layer)
conn_layer.add_to(m)

orig_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Original positions", show=True)
for idx, row in fwy_original_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x], radius=4,
                        color="#E24B4A", fill=True, fill_opacity=0.8,
                        tooltip=f"N: {gdf_nodes.loc[idx, 'N']} (original)").add_to(orig_layer)
orig_layer.add_to(m)

snap_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Snapped positions", show=True)
for idx, row in fwy_snapped_wgs.iterrows():
    rule = fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_rule"]
    folium.CircleMarker(location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x], radius=4,
                        color=RULE_COLORS.get(rule, "#888780"), fill=True, fill_opacity=0.8,
                        tooltip=f"N: {int(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, 'N'])} | {rule} | {fwy_snapped.loc[idx, 'snap_distance_m']}m").add_to(snap_layer)
snap_layer.add_to(m)

folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False).add_to(m)
m
```

```{python}
# Export freeway + interchange debug layers
fwy_snapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"].isin(FWY_IC_RULES)].copy()
fwy_original = gdf_nodes.loc[fwy_snapped.index].copy()

lines = [
    {
        "N": fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "N"],
        "snap_rule": fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_rule"],
        "snap_distance_m": fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_distance_m"],
        "geometry": LineString(
            [fwy_original.loc[idx, "geometry"], fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "geometry"]]
        ),
    }
    for idx in fwy_snapped.index
    if fwy_original.loc[idx, "geometry"].distance(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "geometry"]) > 0
]
gdf_fwy_lines = (
    gpd.GeoDataFrame(lines, crs=CRS_UTM)
    if lines
    else gpd.GeoDataFrame(columns=["N", "snap_rule", "snap_distance_m", "geometry"], crs=CRS_UTM)
)

fwy_unsnapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[
    (is_fwy_node | is_ramp_node) & (gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold")
].copy()

fwy_snapped.to_crs(CRS_WGS).to_file(debug_path, layer="snapped_positions", driver="GPKG")   # WGS84 for export only
fwy_original.to_crs(CRS_WGS).to_file(debug_path, layer="original_positions", driver="GPKG") # WGS84 for export only
if not gdf_fwy_lines.empty:
    gdf_fwy_lines.to_crs(CRS_WGS).to_file(debug_path, layer="displacement_lines", driver="GPKG")  # WGS84 for export only
if not fwy_unsnapped.empty:
    fwy_unsnapped.to_crs(CRS_WGS).to_file(debug_path, layer="unsnapped_nodes", driver="GPKG")     # WGS84 for export only

print(f"Debug package → {debug_path}")
print(f"  snapped_positions:  {len(fwy_snapped):,}")
print(f"    Freeway_Only:     {(fwy_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'Freeway_Only').sum():,}")
print(f"    Freeway_Gore:     {(fwy_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'Freeway_Gore').sum():,}")
print(f"    Interchange_Only: {(fwy_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'Interchange_Only').sum():,}")
print(f"  unsnapped_nodes:    {len(fwy_unsnapped):,}")
```

### D2 — Surface Snapping

Arterial, Collector, and Local nodes snap to surface street endpoints.
`ramp_sf` nodes also land here (intentionally skipped in pass c) — their
`link_directions` cardinals from surface links are the reliable direction signal,
and `gore` endpoints are included in the pool since ramp-to-arterial terminals
can physically sit at freeway/ramp junctions.

```{python}
# Surface pool: surface, ramp_sf, fwy_sf, and gore endpoints.
# gore included because ramp_sf nodes (ramp terminal at arterial) can be co-located
# with a gore point where a freeway and ramp both terminate at grade.
ep_surface = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"].isin(["surface", "ramp_sf", "fwy_sf", "gore"])].copy()
print(f"Surface endpoint pool: {len(ep_surface):,}")
print(f"  surface-only: {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_type'] == 'surface').sum():,}")
print(f"  ramp+surface: {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_type'] == 'ramp_sf').sum():,}")
print(f"  fwy+surface:  {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_type'] == 'fwy_sf').sum():,}")
print(f"  gore:         {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_type'] == 'gore').sum():,}")
```

```{python}
# surface_mask includes ramp_sf nodes (Ramp & Surface, not already snapped)
# so they receive a first-call-wins attempt here with the correct direction col.
surface_mask = (
    (gdf_nodes_snapped["Surface"])
    & (gdf_nodes_snapped["LinkCount"] != 2)
)
print(f"Surface nodes eligible: {surface_mask.sum():,}")

gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped, ep_surface,
    node_mask      = surface_mask,
    max_distance_m = 400,   # wider than pass (c) — ramp_sf terminals can be displaced
    label          = "Surface",
    direction_col  = "link_directions",
    target_id_cols = ["x_round", "y_round"],
)
```

```{python}
surf_dists = gdf_nodes_snapped.loc[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "Surface", "snap_distance_m"]
print(f"Surface nodes snapped:  {len(surf_dists):,}")
print(f"  median: {surf_dists.median():.1f}m  |  max: {surf_dists.max():.1f}m")
print(f"  < 50m:  {(surf_dists < 50).sum():,}  ({(surf_dists < 50).mean()*100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  50-200m: {((surf_dists >= 50) & (surf_dists <= 200)).sum():,}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

# Folium validation map — Surface snapping
surf_snapped_gdf  = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "Surface"]
surf_original_gdf = gdf_nodes.loc[surf_snapped_gdf.index]
surf_snapped_wgs  = surf_snapped_gdf.to_crs(CRS_WGS)
surf_original_wgs = surf_original_gdf.to_crs(CRS_WGS)

connectors_surf = [
    {"geometry": LineString([surf_original_wgs.geometry.loc[idx], surf_snapped_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]]),
     "N": int(surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, "N"]),
     "dist_m": float(surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, "snap_distance_m"])}
    for idx in surf_snapped_gdf.index
    if surf_original_wgs.geometry.loc[idx].distance(surf_snapped_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]) > 0
]
gdf_surf_connectors = gpd.GeoDataFrame(connectors_surf, crs=CRS_WGS)

center = [surf_original_wgs.geometry.y.mean(), surf_original_wgs.geometry.x.mean()]
m_surf = folium.Map(location=center, zoom_start=11, tiles="CartoDB positron")
for _, row in gdf_surf_connectors.iterrows():
    folium.PolyLine([(c[1], c[0]) for c in row.geometry.coords],
                    color="#888780", weight=1, opacity=0.5,
                    tooltip=f"N: {row['N']} | {row['dist_m']}m").add_to(m_surf)
for idx, row in surf_original_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x], radius=3,
                        color="#E24B4A", fill=True, fill_opacity=0.7,
                        tooltip=f"N: {gdf_nodes.loc[idx, 'N']} (original)").add_to(m_surf)
for idx, row in surf_snapped_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x], radius=3,
                        color="#1D9E75", fill=True, fill_opacity=0.7,
                        tooltip=f"N: {int(surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, 'N'])} | {surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, 'snap_distance_m']}m").add_to(m_surf)
folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False).add_to(m_surf)
m_surf
```

### D3 — Transit Snapping

FixedTransit nodes snap to Light Rail (TRAX) and Commuter Rail (FrontRunner)
stops. Bus stops are excluded. Twin platforms merged to station centroid.

```{python}
# Merge co-located platforms (same stop_name) to a single centroid.
# geometry column is plain shapely objects after the lambda — no inherited CRS.
# gdf_rail_stops is already in CRS_UTM (reprojected at import), so set directly.
rail_stops_merged = (
    gdf_rail_stops.groupby("stop_name")
    .apply(
        lambda g: pd.Series(
            {
                "stop_id": ",".join(g["stop_id"].astype(str).unique()),
                "geometry": g.geometry.union_all().centroid,
            }
        ),
        include_groups=False,
    )
    .reset_index()
)
gdf_rail_stops_grouped = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    rail_stops_merged,
    geometry="geometry",
    crs=CRS_UTM,
)

print(f"Grouped rail stops: {len(gdf_rail_stops_grouped):,}")
```

```{python}
transit_dists = gdf_nodes_snapped.loc[
    gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "FixedTransit_Rail", "snap_distance_m"
]
print(f"Transit nodes snapped: {len(transit_dists):,}")
print(f"  median: {transit_dists.median():.1f}m  |  max: {transit_dists.max():.1f}m")
```

---

## Overall Snap Summary

```{python}
print("Results by snap rule:")
print(gdf_nodes_snapped.value_counts("snap_rule").rename("NodeCount").to_frame().to_string())
print(f"\nTotal:              {len(gdf_nodes_snapped):,}")
print(f"Snapped:            {gdf_nodes_snapped['snapped'].sum():,}"
      f"  ({gdf_nodes_snapped['snapped'].mean()*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"Exceeded threshold: {(gdf_nodes_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'exceeded_threshold').sum():,}")
print(f"Not attempted:      {(gdf_nodes_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'none').sum():,}")
```

```{python}
# Exceeded-threshold detail table
bool_cols = [c for c in gdf_nodes_snapped.columns if gdf_nodes_snapped[c].dtype == bool]
gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold"][
    ["N", "snap_distance_m"] + bool_cols
].sort_values("snap_distance_m", ascending=False)
```

### Conflation Performance — KPIs and ECDF

```{python}
snapped_nodes = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snapped"]]

kpi_table = (
    snapped_nodes.groupby("snap_rule")["snap_distance_m"]
    .agg(
        Nodes_Snapped="count",
        Median_Dist_m="median",
        P90_Dist_m=lambda x: np.percentile(x, 90),
        Max_Dist_m="max",
    )
    .round(1)
)
print("=== SNAPPING PERFORMANCE KPIs ===")
print(kpi_table.to_string())
print("\nNodes exceeding threshold:", (gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold").sum())
```

```{python}
COLOR_MAP = {
    "Freeway_Only":      "#1D9E75",
    "Freeway_Gore":      "#534AB7",
    "Interchange_Only":  "#EF9F27",
    "Surface":           "#378ADD",
    "FixedTransit_Rail": "#888780",
}

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")
ax.set_facecolor("white")

for rule, group_data in snapped_nodes.groupby("snap_rule"):
    distances = np.sort(group_data["snap_distance_m"].dropna())
    y = np.arange(1, len(distances) + 1) / len(distances)
    ax.step(distances, y, where="post",
            label=f"{rule} (n={len(distances):,})",
            color=COLOR_MAP.get(rule, "#000000"), linewidth=2)

ax.set_xlim(0, 300)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1.05)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
ax.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.6)
ax.set_title("Algorithm Precision: Cumulative Snapping Distances", fontsize=14, pad=15)
ax.set_xlabel("Displacement Distance (meters)", fontsize=11)
ax.set_ylabel("Percentage of Nodes Snapped", fontsize=11)
ax.legend(loc="lower right", fontsize=10, frameon=True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Export

```{python}
out_path = PATHS["out_node_snapped"]
out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_nodes_snapped.to_crs(CRS_WGS).to_file(out_path, driver="GPKG")
print(f"Snapped nodes saved to: {out_path}")
```
